message:
fix(runtime_compat): mark select! macro_export and re-export from crate root

Follow-up to the visibility change in the preceding commit. The earlier
`pub use select` import at the module level did not actually make the
macro reachable from outside the crate, because Rust's macro-2.0-lite
rules route `macro_rules!` through the crate root; a bare `pub use
select` from inside `runtime_compat` attempts to re-export a local name
that is still scoped to that module.

Two small changes restore working external access:

1. Annotate the `macro_rules! select { ... }` definition with
   `#[macro_export]` so it is hoisted into the crate root namespace
   (`frankenterm_core::select!`) at expansion time.
2. Change the module-level re-export from `pub use select` to
   `pub use crate::select`, so
   `frankenterm_core::runtime_compat::select!` resolves to the
   hoisted crate-root version. This preserves the existing call-site
   spelling `frankenterm_core::runtime_compat::select! { ... }` that
   `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`, test files, and the robot-SDK
   transport all use.

Macro body is byte-identical — same `biased;`, `<pat> = <fut> =>
<body>` (block and expression), and no-biased three-arm variants.
Behaviour unchanged for in-crate callers.
